**Difficulty:** Easy (good first issue!)

### 🎯 Your Task

Add this trivia question to our growing quiz bank!

### The Trivia Question

**Question:** Which month is traditionally associated with cherry blossom viewing (hanami) in Japan? (Community variation 3)

**Answers:**
1. January
2. April
3. July
4. October

**Correct Answer Index:** 1

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/japan-trivia-easy.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japan-trivia-easy.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following trivia object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "question": "Which month is traditionally associated with cherry blossom viewing (hanami) in Japan? (Community variation 3)",
  "difficulty": "easy",
  "answers": [
    "January",
    "April",
    "July",
    "October"
  ],
  "correctIndex": 1
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add new trivia question`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

## Add Japanese Cultural Etiquette Tip

**Category:** Community Contribution - Cultural Etiquette  
**Difficulty:** Easy (good first issue!)  
**Estimated Time:** <1 min

---

### Your Task

Add this etiquette tip to help learners navigate Japanese social situations confidently!

### The Etiquette Tip

**Situation:** お辞儀 2

- Do: 場面に応じて角度を使い分ける
- Avoid: 笑いながら浅く頭を下げる

> **Note:** 丁寧さは姿勢で伝わる。

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/japanese-cultural-etiquette.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japanese-cultural-etiquette.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following cultural etiquette object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "situation": "お辞儀 2",
  "do": "場面に応じて角度を使い分ける",
  "dont": "笑いながら浅く頭を下げる",
  "note": "丁寧さは姿勢で伝わる。"
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add new cultural etiquette tip`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

## 🎬 Add Famous Anime Quote

**Category:** Community Contribution - Anime Quote  
**Difficulty:** Easy (good first issue!)  
**Estimated Time:** <1 min

---

### 🎯 Your Task

Add this iconic anime quote so learners can enjoy Japanese pop culture!

### The Quote

| Japanese | Romaji | English |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **仲間を大切にしない奴はそれ以上のクズだ** | Nakama o taisetsu ni shinai yatsu wa sore ijou no kuzu da | Those who don't take care of their comrades are worse than scum. (alt wording 34) |

**Anime:** Naruto  
**Character:** Kakashi Hatake

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/anime-quotes.json`](../blob/main/community/content/anime-quotes.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following anime quote object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "japanese": "仲間を大切にしない奴はそれ以上のクズだ",
  "romaji": "Nakama o taisetsu ni shinai yatsu wa sore ijou no kuzu da",
  "english": "Those who don't take care of their comrades are worse than scum. (alt wording 34)",
  "anime": "Naruto",
  "character": "Kakashi Hatake"
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add anime quote`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

Changes have been made to enhance debugging productivity using the Gemini CLI DevTools. 

Enhancements include:
- Network UI filters with RegEx, text highlighting, toggle-buttons for methods and status of requests
- Similar filter upgrades in Console for logs
- Users can now filter specific HTTP requests, error payloads, and Regex pattern strings simultaneously with matched strings visually highlighted.


## Details

- **UI filtering**: added a `FilterBar` component. Search inputs will now dynamically drill down url logs (in console, and network tab). Users can also make use of RegEx for their searches. Network methods, and Status Codes are also filterable (only appear as required). 
- **server autostart**: automatically detects when it acts as the primary runtime entry script and correctly hooks onto the 25417. Faced some issues when using `npm run start` and this seemed to be the fix.

I see 11 GPUs available:

<img width="373" height="538" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e8994fcf-67fb-4407-b7e4-e3f18d644bff" />

and

<img width="1067" height="526" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4bbb7c37-e520-41f7-80fb-a77ed018ff3c" />

But actually only 3 are available _to me_. 8 are reserved for use by one guy (who isn't using them right now, so it is easy to see), via a taint on one node.

```console
me@mymac kubestellar % kubectl get node fmaas-vllm-d-wv25b-worker-h100-3-4l5t5 -o jsonpath='{.spec.taints}' | jq .
[
  {
    "effect": "NoSchedule",
    "key": "dedicated",
    "value": "ofer"
  }
]
```

It would be nice if these displays were sensitive to taints, so that they do not give me a false impression of what is available to me.

### Proposed Solution

In each of those two status blocks or cards, have a control that has a checkbox for taints to tolerate.

### Feature Area

Dashboard / Cards

## Add Japanese Cultural Etiquette Tip

**Category:** Community Contribution - Cultural Etiquette  
**Difficulty:** Easy (good first issue!)  
**Estimated Time:** <1 min

---

### Your Task

Add this etiquette tip to help learners navigate Japanese social situations confidently!

### The Etiquette Tip

**Situation:** 訪問手土産 1

- Do: 個包装のお菓子を持参する
- Avoid: 匂いの強いものを持参する

> **Note:** 分けやすさが好まれる。

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/japanese-cultural-etiquette.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japanese-cultural-etiquette.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following cultural etiquette object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "situation": "訪問手土産 1",
  "do": "個包装のお菓子を持参する",
  "dont": "匂いの強いものを持参する",
  "note": "分けやすさが好まれる。"
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add new cultural etiquette tip`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

## Add Famous Japanese Video Game Quote

**Category:** Community Contribution - Video Game Quote  
**Difficulty:** Easy (good first issue!)  
**Estimated Time:** <1 min

---

### Your Task

Add this iconic Japanese game quote so learners can enjoy game culture while studying!

### The Video Game Quote

| Japanese | Romaji | English |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **俺は悪くねぇ！** | Ore wa warukunee! | It's not my fault! |

**Game:** Tales of the Abyss  
**Character:** Luke fon Fabre

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/japanese-videogame-quotes.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japanese-videogame-quotes.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following video game quote object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "japanese": "俺は悪くねぇ！",
  "romaji": "Ore wa warukunee!",
  "english": "It's not my fault!",
  "game": "Tales of the Abyss",
  "character": "Luke fon Fabre"
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add video game quote`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!
